topic.name: Wildlife Conservation Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts aimed at the conservation, management, and regulation of wildlife and their habitats in the United States.

Document 1: Wildlife Veterinarians Employment and Training Act
wildlife disease research and training, disease surveillance, ecology and wildlife population management
Document 2: Freedom from Over-Criminalization and Unjust Seizures Act of 2012
prohibition on importing, exporting, transporting, selling, receiving, acquiring, or purchasing fish or wildlife taken in violation of foreign law
Document 3: Jonas Salk Elementary Cap Undo Regulatory Environmental Delay or CURED Act
issues a permit under the Endangered Species Act
Document 4: Invasive Fish and Wildlife Prevention Act of 2012
regulation of nonnative wildlife taxa
Document 5: Salamander Community Conservation Act
amends the Endangered Species Act
Document 6: Farmers Protection Act of 2012
amends the Migratory Bird Treaty Act
Document 7: National Wildlife Refuge System Administration Act of 1966
designate a portion of the Arctic National Wildlife Refuge
Document 8: Migratory Bird Treaty Amendment Act of 2011
allows taking migratory game birds by the aid of baiting
Document 9: Big Cats and Public Safety Protection Act
prohibit importing, exporting, transporting, selling, or possessing prohibited wildlife species

topic.name: sustainable energy solutions
topic.description: The documents discuss various approaches and technologies aimed at promoting sustainable energy production and consumption.

doc3: impact of sustainable practices on climate change

topic.name: sustainable energy solutions
topic.description: Exploration of innovative and eco-friendly energy technologies and practices.

doc2: impact of wind energy on local economies

topic.name: Medicare Legislation and Reforms
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts aimed at modifying and improving the Medicare program and related health services in the United States.

Document 1: Medicare Payment Rate Disclosure Act of 2012
makes publicly available Medicare payment rates for hospital inpatient and outpatient procedures
Document 2: Medicare Advantage Program Integrity Act of 2012
revises the coding intensity adjustment factor for payments to Medicare+Choice organizations
Document 4: Medicare Firewall Act of 2011
defund multi-stakeholder groups for health care quality measures
Document 5: STEAM Act of 2011
repeal administrative penalties for non-successful electronic prescribers
Document 6: Ambulatory Surgical Center Quality and Access Act of 2011
requires alignment of updates for ASC services with hospital outpatient department services

topic.name: Mortgage and Tax Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative measures related to mortgages, homeownership, and associated tax implications.

Document 1: Amends the National Housing Act and the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation Act
establish a program to encourage the development of a deliverable residential mortgage futures market.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend the exclusion from gross income of income attributable to the discharge of indebtedness on a principal residence
extend through 2012 the tax deduction for mortgage insurance premiums.
Document 3: Amends the Credit Repair Organizations Act to exempt from its coverage any consumer reporting agency
Directs the Federal Trade Commission to study whether... any other person should be exempt.
Document 4: Amends the Credit Repair Organizations Act to exempt from its coverage any consumer reporting agency
Preempts state law and regulations concerning a credit repair organization.
Document 5: This bill amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a refundable tax credit equal to 50% of the excess home mortgage principal payments
The credit is limited to $500 per year.
Document 6: This bill amends the Internal Revenue Code to provide for tax-preferred savings accounts for first-time homebuyers
Distributions from the account that are used to pay the qualified principal residence purchase expenditures of the designated beneficiary are excluded from gross income.
Document 7: This bill amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through December 31, 2017, the tax deduction for mortgage insurance premiums treated as interest.
Document 8: This bill amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through December 31, 2018, the tax deduction for mortgage insurance premiums treated as interest.
Document 9: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to increase by 25% the allowable tax deduction for mortgage interest on a principal residence
waive the requirement to repay credit amounts under the first-time homebuyer tax credit.

topic.name: Temporary Suspension of Tariffs
topic.description: The documents discuss amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States regarding the temporary suspension or extension of duties on various specific materials and products.

Document 1: suspend temporarily the duty on cellular plastic membrane sheets
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 2: suspend temporarily the duty on certain woven mesh fabric
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 3: suspend temporarily the duty on certain plastic fittings
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 4: extend the temporary suspension of duty on certain untwisted filament yarns
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 5: extend the temporary suspension of duty on certain synthetic filament yarns
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 6: extend the temporary suspension of duty on aqueous dispersions
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 7: extend the temporary suspension of duty on aqueous catalytic preparations
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 8: extend temporarily the suspension of duty on 3 Chloro 4 Methyl Aniline
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 9: extend the temporary suspension of duty on noils of camel hair
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 10: extend temporarily the reduction of duty on such footwear
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States

topic.name: National Security Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing national security through intelligence sharing, military operations, and personnel management.

Document 1: Amends the National Security Act of 1947
direct the head of an element of the intelligence community to provide intelligence information
Document 2: Prohibits the Attorney General from initiating a custodial interrogation
requires consultation with intelligence officials before actions against foreign persons
Document 3: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report on foreign countries relating to EMP weapons
Document 4: Prohibits the Attorney General from initiating a custodial interrogation
requires consultation with intelligence officials
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of Defense to establish a board of review for servicemembers' mental health
Document 6: Prohibits operations involving armed unmanned aerial vehicles by federal agencies other than DOD
Document 7: Requires amounts made available under the Iraq Security Forces Fund to be provided as loans
Document 8: Directs the Department of Defense to enhance security of military personnel at installations
Document 9: Directs the Secretary of the Air Force to maintain current leadership rank and capabilities at Eielson Air Force Base
Document 10: Requires the Secretary of Defense to transfer members of the Armed Forces to the Secretary of Veterans Affairs for outpatient care

topic.name: Land Conveyance and Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts related to the conveyance, management, and preservation of federal and non-federal lands for environmental conservation, habitat preservation, and public use.

Document 1: convey to the Ranch all right, title, and interest to such land
modifies the boundaries of the Grand Staircase-Escalante National Monument
Document 2: authorize the Secretary of Agriculture to accept the offer
the non-federal land to become part of BLM's Shoshone District
Document 3: authorize the permitting authority to issue a Good Samaritan discharge permit
to carry out a project to remediate an inactive or abandoned mine site
Document 4: grants the city of Mesquite, Nevada, the exclusive right to purchase certain parcels of public land
proceeds from certain sales to be used for the implementation of the Virgin River Multispecies Habitat Conservation Plan
Document 5: Requires the exchange of certain federal and non-federal lands
establishment under this Act of the Lesser Prairie Chicken Habitat Preservation Area
Document 6: directs the Secretary of Agriculture and the Secretary of the Interior to convey specified federal lands
to be managed in accordance with the terms of the Recreation and Public Purposes Act
Document 7: transfers administrative jurisdiction over the Joliet Training Area
requires the deed for such land to contain restrictive covenants
Document 8: sets forth requirements concerning the maintenance of viable populations of existing native and desired non-native species
adopt and implement a comprehensive monitoring program
Document 9: permits Clark County, Nevada, to convey approximately 51 acres of land
provides for the reversion of such land to Clark County
Document 10: authorizes the Boy Scouts to convey to Brian Head Resort certain public land
in exchange for conveyance by Brian Head Resort of certain of its private land

topic.name: Veterans' Benefits and Support
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures aimed at enhancing benefits and support services for veterans, including disability compensation, survivor benefits, and addressing issues related to domestic violence and mental health.

Document 1: Extends the authorized concurrent receipt of disability severance pay from the Department of Defense (DOD) and compensation for the same disability under any law administered by the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) to include all veterans with a combat-related disability.
Makes such amendment effective as of January 28, 2008.
Document 2: Reduces from age 57 to age 55 the age after which the remarriage of the surviving spouse of a deceased veteran shall not result in termination of dependency and indemnity compensation otherwise payable to that surviving spouse.
Prohibits payment of retroactive benefits.
Document 5: Establishes in the Department of Defense (DOD) an Office of the Victims' Advocate to facilitate access to services for victims of domestic or family violence, sexual assault, and stalking in the military.
Specifies the rights of victims of such violence reported to a military law enforcement agency.
Document 6: Defines 'combat with the enemy' for purposes of proof of service-connection for veterans' disability compensation, as service on active duty in a theater of combat operations during a period of war.
In combat against a hostile force during a period of hostilities.
Document 10: Makes permanent the authority of the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) to furnish hospital care, medical services, and nursing home care for any illness to a veteran who participated in a test conducted by the Department of Defense (DOD).
Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to contract with the Institute of Medicine of the National Academies to conduct an expanded study on the health impact of Project SHAD.

topic.name: U.S. Immigration Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts and amendments related to immigration policies and regulations in the United States.

Document 1: Development, Relief, and Education for Alien Minors Act of 2010 or DREAM Act of 2010
Authorizes the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) to cancel the removal of, and adjust to conditional nonimmigrant status, an alien who entered the United States before his or her 16th birthday.
Document 2: Criminal Alien Accountability Act
Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to establish minimum imprisonment terms for specified categories of aliens.
Document 3: Fallen Heroes Family Act of 2011
Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to include as a nonimmigrant alien an alien who is the parent of a child who is a U.S. citizen.
Document 4: Birthright Citizenship Act of 2011
Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to consider a person born in the United States 'subject to the jurisdiction' of the United States for citizenship at birth purposes.
Document 5: Amends the Illegal Immigration Reform and Immigrant Responsibility Act of 1996
Requires federal contractors and subcontractors to participate in the E-Verify Program.
Document 6: Stopping Trained in America Ph.D.s From Leaving the Economy Act of 2011
Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to exempt from direct numerical limitations aliens who have earned a Ph.D. degree in STEM.
Document 7: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to allow states to be compensated for incarcerating undocumented aliens
Document 8: Nuclear Family Priority Act
Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to replace existing family-sponsored immigrant categories.
Document 9: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to eliminate the diversity immigrant program
Provides an equal number of annual immigrant visas to certain employment-based aliens.
Document 10: Liberian Refugee Immigration Fairness Act of 2011
Adjusts to permanent resident status a qualifying Liberian national who has been continuously present in the United States.

topic.name: Drug Policy Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative efforts to amend drug control laws, particularly regarding marijuana and opioids, addressing issues of regulation, advertising, and public health.

1: Ending Federal Marijuana Prohibition Act of 2015
removes marijuana and tetrahydrocannabinols from the list of Schedule I controlled substances.
2: amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to authorize the amendment or repeal of an over-the-counter drug monograph.
3: Dangerous Synthetic Drug Control Act of 2016
adds certain synthetic opioids, hallucinogens, and cannabinoids to schedule I.
4: provides support for activities related to opioid abuse, including treatment services.
5: Marijuana Advertising in Legal States Act of 2016
modifies the application of a provision that limits advertisements with respect to certain controlled substances.
6: Stop Meth Labs and Enhance Patient Access Act of 2015
revises the standard used by the DEA to determine whether to exempt a scheduled listed chemical product.
7: requires the Department of Health and Human Services to convene an interagency task force to develop best practices for pain management.

topic.name: Transportation Safety Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing safety standards and procedures in various modes of transportation, including aviation and rail.

Document 1: Requires railroad carriers engaged in the transportation of hazardous materials to submit to the Secretary of Transportation a plan for providing at least 48 hours notification to requesting local emergency responders before transporting hazmats.
Directs the Secretary to require carriers to demonstrate periodically that the plan's procedures are adequate.
Document 2: Directs the Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to implement certain aviation safety recommendations of the National Transportation Safety Board (NTSB).
Requires the Administrator to perform, at least annually, random, unannounced, onsite inspections of regional air carriers to ensure their compliance with FAA safety standards.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security to issue regulations to limit the size of baggage and personal items carried on board a passenger aircraft.
Requires the TSA to notify passengers via their websites of federal carry-on baggage and personal item limitations.
Document 6: Requires the Secretary of Transportation to revise certain airline service quality performance reporting regulations to require air carriers to disclose the nature and source of flight delays and cancellations experienced by air travelers.
Document 8: Requires each air carrier and airport operator to submit for approval by the Secretary of Transportation a proposed contingency plan meeting minimum standards established by the Secretary.
Authorizes the Secretary to assess a civil penalty against air carriers and airport operators that fail to submit, obtain approval of, or adhere to a contingency plan.

topic.name: Labor Rights and Employment Regulations
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative measures aimed at protecting labor rights, regulating employment practices, and ensuring fair treatment of employees in the workplace.

Document 1: Requires the screening of passengers on flights... by employees of a private screening company.
Grants airport operators and private screening companies the authority to employ and terminate passenger screeners.
Document 2: Requires a labor organization... to provide all employees covered by a collective bargaining agreement with written notice explaining how it calculated the share of such dues or fees related to non-political costs.
Prohibits a labor organization from collecting dues or fees from non-union employees without their consent.
Document 4: Amends federal arbitration law to exclude from the definition of 'commerce' all contracts of employment.
Defines 'employment dispute' as a dispute between an employer and employee arising out of the employer-employee relationship.
Document 5: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to prohibit the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) from requiring an employer to provide the NLRB or a labor organization with employee telephone numbers or email addresses.
Document 6: Prohibits the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) from approving an employer petition for any visa authorizing employment in the United States unless the employer has certified to the Secretary of Labor.
States that if an employer provides a notice of a mass layoff... any visas approved during the most recent 12-month period for such employer shall expire...
Document 7: Amends the Worker Adjustment and Retraining Notification Act... requires an employer to give 90-day written notice... before ordering a plant closing or mass layoff.
Makes an employer who violates such notice requirements liable to the employee for... two days pay.
Document 10: Amends the Civil Rights Act of 1964 to declare that an unlawful employment practice occurs when... a discriminatory compensation decision or other practice is adopted.
Allows liability to accrue, and allows an aggrieved person to obtain relief, including recovery of back pay.

topic.name: Facility Designation Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures that designate and name various public facilities across the United States.

Document 1: Designates the facility of the Grandfather Ranger District of the U.S. Forest Service as the 'Jason Crisp Forest Service Building.'
Document 2: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service as the 'Harold George Bennett Post Office.'
Document 3: Designates the Department of Veterans Affairs community-based outpatient clinic as the 'Loren R. Kaufman Memorial Veterans' Clinic.'
Document 4: Designates the Department of Veterans Affairs medical center in Waco, Texas, as the 'Doris Miller Department of Veterans Affairs Medical Center.'
Document 5: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service as the 'Sergeant Shawn T. Hannon and Master Sergeant Jeffrey J. Rieck and Veterans Memorial Post Office Building.'
Document 6: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service as the 'Father Richard Marquess-Barry Post Office Building.'
Document 7: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service as the 'Cynthia Jenkins Post Office Building.'
Document 9: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service as the 'Corporal Juan Mariel Alcantara Post Office Building.'

topic.name: Small Business Legislation
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at supporting and regulating small businesses in the United States.

Document 1: Extends through July 31, 2011, under the same terms and conditions, the authorization for any program, authority, or provision, including any pilot program, that is currently authorized through May 31, 2011, under the Small Business Act or the Small Business Investment Act of 1958.
Reauthorize through FY2011 the Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) and Small Business Technology Transfer (STTR) programs of the Small Business Administration (SBA).
Document 3: Directs each federal agency that provides contracts, grants, loans, or loan guarantees to businesses to require, as a condition of such assistance, that such businesses provide annually information on the number of employees employed in the United States.
Prohibits, until the situation is corrected, subsequent federal assistance to businesses that lay off a greater percentage of workers in the United States and its territories than in other countries.
Document 5: Amends the Regulatory Flexibility Act (RFA) to revise the regulatory process (rulemaking) with respect to small entities.
Requires each agency to establish a plan for the periodic review (every eight years) of its rules that have a significant adverse economic impact on small entities.
Document 7: Amends the Small Business Investment Act of 1958 to increase from $225 million to $350 million the maximum amount of outstanding leverage to be made available by the Small Business Administration (SBA).
Document 9: Directs the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) to modify its regulations under the Sarbanes-Oxley Act of 2002 (SOX) for annual management assessments of, and reports on, internal financial controls.

Requires such regulations to exempt a non-accelerated filer of a required annual report from the requirement that each registered public accounting firm that prepares or issues the audit report for a securities issuer attest to, and report on, the assessment by the issuer's management of the effectiveness of its internal control over financial reporting.

topic.name: U.S. Postal Service Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the operations, employee rights, and commemorative activities of the U.S. Postal Service.

Document 1: honors the sacrifices of the members of the Armed Forces
depicts the National League of Families POW/MIA flag
Document 2: replace at least 10% of the gasoline-powered motor vehicles
ensure that at least 75% of the Postal fleet is comprised of such electric motor vehicles
Document 3: ensure reasonable and sustainable workloads and schedules for supervisory and management employees
consultation process for changes in pay policies
Document 4: extends federal employee protections against adverse personnel actions
right to appeal such actions to the Merit Systems Protection Board
Document 5: recalculation of annuities for employees of the U.S. Postal Service
eliminates the requirement for pre-funding of the Postal Service Retiree Health Benefits Fund
Document 6: issue a commemorative postage stamp in honor of Ebenezer D. Bassett
Document 7: prohibits mail processing facilities from being closed or consolidated
Document 8: ensure reasonable and sustainable workloads and schedules for supervisory and management employees
changes in pay policies and schedules
Document 9: removes the authority of the USPS to issue semipostal stamps
Congress must determine the appropriate agencies or entities that receive such amounts
Document 10: make it a crime to use the mail to publish personally identifiable information
subject to criminal penalties and civil liability

topic.name: Campaign Finance Reform
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating campaign financing and preventing foreign influence in elections.

Document 1: require the treasurer of each authorized political committee to establish a separate account for contributions
Prohibits the use of amounts in such account to make disbursements
Document 2: require the identification of funding and authorizing sources in communications
Prescribes special rules for express advocacy radio and television communications
Document 3: prohibit the conversion of leadership PAC funds to personal use
Document 4: prohibit a corporation from making any independent expenditure without obtaining prior approval of shareholders
Subjects officers and directors of a corporation to personal liability
Document 5: apply the ban on contributions and expenditures by foreign nationals to domestic corporations
percentage of outstanding shares controlled by foreign principals
Document 6: apply the ban on contributions and expenditures by foreign nationals to domestic corporations whose shareholders include foreign principals
Document 7: apply the ban to domestic corporations with foreign principals on the board of directors
apply the ban to subsidiaries of foreign corporations
Document 8: prohibit corporations from making any expenditure if they employ a registered lobbyist
Document 9: apply the prohibition against conversion to personal use of contributions to political committees
allow the use of contributions for authorized expenditures

topic.name: Child Care and Abortion Legislation
topic.description: Legislation addressing child care funding and regulations alongside abortion-related restrictions and parental involvement requirements.

1: Amends the Child Care and Development Block Grant Act of 1990
Requires the state plan to demonstrate the manner in which the state will meet the specific child care needs of low-income and working families.
2: Prohibits federal funding of any affiliate, subsidiary, successor, or clinic of Planned Parenthood Federation of America
Department of Justice must investigate whether Planned Parenthood has engaged in illegal activity pertaining to fetal tissue products.
3: Prohibits, for a one-year period, the availability of federal funds for any purpose to Planned Parenthood
Requires certification that affiliates will not perform abortions during such period.
4: Prohibits transporting a minor child across a state line to obtain an abortion
Makes it an affirmative defense to a prosecution that a defendant reasonably believed that the required parental consent took place.
9: Amends the Child Care and Development Block Grant Act of 1990 to revise the requirements for a state plan
Requires minimum health and safety training appropriate to a child care provider mandatory under state or local law.

topic.name: Federal Land Management and Use
topic.description: Legislation focused on the management, exchange, and use of federal lands for various purposes including conservation, education, and development.

1: Authorize the Secretary of Agriculture to convey specified National Forest System (NFS) lands
ensure public access to the NFS lands.
2: designate specified federal land in Nevada as a solar pilot project area
ensure the sound, efficient, and environmentally responsible development of solar resources.
3: offer for disposal by competitive sale certain federal lands
previously identified as suitable for disposal.
4: carry out eligible projects on National Forest System lands
focus on surface, ladder, and canopy fuels reduction activities.
5: Makes grants of land to states for the support of public education
proceeds to be used only for the support of public education.
8: convey to the Powell Recreation District in Wyoming
for use as a shooting range.
9: Withdraws approximately 1,068,908 acres of the Tusayan Ranger District
from entry, appropriation, and disposal under the public land laws.

topic.name: Lobbying and Firearm Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to lobbying disclosure and regulations surrounding firearm possession and use in federal lands.

Document 1: Requires the Attorney General to establish the Lobbying Disclosure Act Enforcement Task Force.
Amends the Lobbying Disclosure Act of 1995 to require notifications of noncompliance of lobbyist disclosure requirements.
Document 2: Prohibits a former Member of Congress or former congressional employee who is a registered lobbyist from being eligible for any benefits.
States that nothing in this Act shall be considered to prevent the payment of any CSRS or FERS lump-sum credit.
Document 4: Prohibits any federal regulation from restricting any individual from possessing or carrying a firearm if that restriction is based upon the fact that the individual is in a unit of the National Park System.
Document 5: Prohibits any federal regulation from restricting any individual from possessing or carrying a firearm or ammunition, if such restriction is based, in whole or in part, upon the fact that the individual is in a unit of the National Park System.
Document 6: Amends the federal criminal code to provide for reciprocity for the carrying of certain concealed firearms in different states.
Carrying a valid state license or permit for carrying a concealed firearm.

topic.name: Energy Sector Sanctions
topic.description: The documents primarily discuss various U.S. legislative acts aimed at imposing sanctions on countries and entities involved in the energy sector, particularly focusing on petroleum and financial transactions.

Document 1: Prohibits entry into contracts for petroleum development in Iraq
Sets forth penalties for violation of such prohibitions.
Document 2: U.S. policy to encourage foreign governments to cease investment in Iran's energy sector
Impose sanctions on the Central Bank of Iran.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to determine if Iranian oil companies are affiliated with the IRGC
Applies prohibitions on U.S. correspondent accounts for foreign financial institutions engaging with Iranian petroleum.
Document 8: Directs the President to impose sanctions against persons conducting transactions with Iranian entities in the energy sector
Exempts certain crude oil purchases from Iran under specified conditions.

topic.name: Legal Reforms and Health Policies
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legal reforms related to health, employment, and social justice, encompassing various legislative acts aimed at improving public health, protecting rights, and ensuring compliance with legal obligations.

Document 1: Amends the federal criminal code to impose a fine and/or a prison term of up to two years on any person who travels in interstate or foreign commerce to evade compliance with a court-ordered property distribution in a divorce proceeding.
Requires payment of restitution equal to the total unpaid property distribution from any person convicted of evading payment.
Document 2: Prohibits the federal government from preventing the use of a claim describing any nutrient in a food or dietary supplement as mitigating, treating, or preventing any disease.
Revokes all FDA rules prohibiting nutrient-disease relationship claims.
Document 3: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require the Director of the National Cancer Institute to enter into cooperative agreements with, or make grants to, public or nonprofit entities to establish and operate Ovarian Cancer Biomarker Centers of Excellence.
Permits federal funds to be used for research on the development and characterization of new biomarkers for ovarian cancer.
Document 4: Declares that it is the purpose of this Act to ensure that American workers are allowed to follow, without financial harm, employer and public health authority recommendations to stay home when they have symptoms of a contagious disease.
Requires an employer to provide paid sick leave to an employee for each workday, or portion of workday, that the employer directs the employee to leave work.
Document 5: Amends the Solid Waste Disposal Act to provide that no person shall be liable under any federal, state, or local law because an underground storage tank is not compatible with any fuel or fuel additive.
Directs the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to issue regulations setting standards for determining compatibility.
Document 6: Amends the Solid Waste Disposal Act to provide that no person shall be liable under any federal, state, or local law because an underground storage tank is not compatible with any fuel or fuel additive.
Deems tanks, systems, and equipment that have been listed by a nationally recognized testing laboratory as compatible.
Document 7: Amends the Americans with Disabilities Act of 1990 to deny jurisdiction to a state or federal court in a civil action a plaintiff commences for remedies for disability discrimination unless the plaintiff notified the defendant in writing of the alleged violations.
Requires a remedial period of 90 days after the notice.
Document 8: Directs the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) to convene a Pain Management Best Practices Interagency Task Force.
Authorizes the Attorney General to make grants to address drug abuse, including educational efforts and communitywide strategies.
Document 9: Amends the federal criminal code to impose a fine and/or a prison term of up to two years on any person who travels in interstate or foreign commerce to evade compliance with a court-ordered property distribution in a divorce proceeding.
Requires payment of restitution equal to the total unpaid property distribution from any person convicted of evading payment.
Document 10: Amends the Age Discrimination in Employment Act of 1967 to specify that an unlawful employment practice is established when the complaining party demonstrates that age was a motivating factor for any unlawful employment practice.
Permits a complaining party to rely on any type or form of admissible evidence.

topic.name: Federal Accountability and Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing transparency, accountability, and oversight in federal spending and programs.

Document 1: Requires the Secretary of Homeland Security to designate Haiti as a country whose qualifying nationals may be eligible for temporary protected status.
Document 3: Requires a state, a local government, a nonprofit organization, or a private entity to conduct an audit using an independent external auditor of any program, project, or activity it administers that is funded through an earmark.
Document 4: Requires each federal department or independent establishment to submit to Congress a report that contains an evaluation of contractor compliance with the provisions of that Act.
Document 5: Requires the Director of the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) to establish a publicly available website for tracking, in detail, all expenditures of funds over $500.
Document 8: Directs the President to modify and update the recovery.gov website to ensure that states, cities, and other general purpose political subdivisions can easily ascertain eligibility for funds.

topic.name: Veterans and Renewable Energy Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts related to the recognition of veterans and the development of renewable energy technologies and infrastructure in the United States.

Document 1: Korean War Veterans Recognition Act of 2008
adds National Korean War Veterans Armistice Day, July 27, to the list of days on which the flag of the United States should be displayed.
Document 4: Biofuels Research and Development Enhancement Act

directs the Secretary of Energy to establish a technology transfer center to disseminate information on research, development, and commercial application of technologies related to specified biofuels.

topic.name: Great Lakes Conservation Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the protection and management of the Great Lakes ecosystem, particularly addressing invasive species and wildlife conservation.

Document 1: requires federal agencies to take immediate actions to prevent the upstream transfer of aquatic nuisance species from the Mississippi River basin to the Great Lakes basin
focus on Asian carp species and other aquatic nuisance species of concern
Document 2: requires the Department of the Interior to reissue the final rule that removed the gray wolf in the Western Great Lakes Distinct Population Segment from the List of Endangered and Threatened Wildlife
Document 3: prohibits any wolf of the species Canis lupus in Minnesota, Wisconsin, and Michigan from being treated under any status of the Endangered Species Act
exclusive jurisdiction over the management of those wolves within its borders
Document 4: requires actions to prevent the transfer of aquatic invasive species, with a focus on Asian carp species
directs the EPA Administrator to coordinate with local authorities to carry out engineering and construction of flood mitigation
Document 5: requires NOAA to create an online database of research and information on the causes and corrective actions being taken with regard to algal blooms in the Great Lakes
Document 6: prohibits the importation into or shipment within the United States of the species of mussels of the genus Dreissena
currently applies to only the zebra mussel
Document 8: requires actions to prevent the transfer of aquatic invasive species, with a focus on Asian carp species
directs the EPA Administrator to coordinate with local authorities to carry out engineering and construction of flood mitigation
